name: texttable

metadata.name: texttable
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: module to create simple ASCII tables

documentation:
# texttable

Python module to create simple ASCII tables

## Availability

This module is available on [PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/texttable/), and has been packaged for several Linux/Unix platforms
([Debian](https://packages.debian.org/search?&searchon=names&keywords=python-texttable+),
[FreeBSD](https://www.freebsd.org/cgi/ports.cgi?query=texttable&stype=all), Fedora, Suse...).

## Dependencies

If available, [cjkwrap](https://github.com/fgallaire/cjkwrap) library is used instead of textwrap, for a better wrapping of CJK text.

If available, [wcwidth](https://github.com/jquast/wcwidth) library is used for a better rendering (basic emoji support).

## Documentation

```
NAME
    texttable - module to create simple ASCII tables

FILE
    /usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/texttable.py

DESCRIPTION

    Example:

        table = Texttable()
        table.set_cols_align(["l", "r", "c"])
        table.set_cols_valign(["t", "m", "b"])
        table.add_rows([["Name", "Age", "Nickname"],
                        ["Mr\nXavier\nHuon", 32, "Xav'"],
                        ["Mr\nBaptiste\nClement", 1, "Baby"],
                        ["Mme\nLouise\nBourgeau", 28, "Lou\n\nLoue"]])
        print(table.draw())
        print()

        table = Texttable()
        table.set_deco(Texttable.HEADER)
        table.set_cols_dtype(['t',  # text
                              'f',  # float (decimal)
                              'e',  # float (exponent)
                              'i',  # integer
                              'a']) # automatic
        table.set_cols_align(["l", "r", "r", "r", "l"])
        table.add_rows([["text",    "float", "exp", "int", "auto"],
                        ["abcd",    "67",    654,   89,    128.001],
                        ["efghijk", 67.5434, .654,  89.6,  12800000000000000000000.00023],
                        ["lmn",     5e-78,   5e-78, 89.4,  .000000000000128],
                        ["opqrstu", .023,    5e+78, 92.,   12800000000000000000000]])
        print(table.draw())

    Result:

        +----------+-----+----------+
        |   Name   | Age | Nickname |
        +==========+=====+==========+
        | Mr       |     |          |
        | Xavier   |  32 |          |
        | Huon     |     |   Xav'   |
        +----------+-----+----------+
        | Mr       |     |          |
        | Baptiste |   1 |          |
        | Clement  |     |   Baby   |
        +----------+-----+----------+
        | Mme      |     |   Lou    |
        | Louise   |  28 |          |
        | Bourgeau |     |   Loue   |
        +----------+-----+----------+

         text     float       exp      int     auto
        ==============================================
        abcd      67.000   6.540e+02    89   128.001
        efghijk   67.543   6.540e-01    90   1.280e+22
        lmn        0.000   5.000e-78    89   0.000
        opqrstu    0.023   5.000e+78    92   1.280e+22

CLASSES
    class Texttable
     |  Methods defined here:
     |
     |  __init__(self, max_width=80)
     |      Constructor
     |
     |      - max_width is an integer, specifying the maximum width of the table
     |      - if set to 0, size is unlimited, therefore cells won't be wrapped
     |
     |  add_row(self, array)
     |      Add a row in the rows stack
     |
     |      - cells can contain newlines and tabs
     |
     |  add_rows(self, rows, header=True)
     |      Add several rows in the rows stack
     |
     |      - The 'rows' argument can be either an iterator returning arrays,
     |        or a by-dimensional array
     |      - 'header' specifies if the first row should be used as the header
     |        of the table
     |
     |  draw(self)
     |      Draw the table
     |
     |      - the table is returned as a whole string
     |
     |  header(self, array)
     |      Specify the header of the table
     |
     |  reset(self)
     |      Reset the instance
     |
     |      - reset rows and header
     |
     |  set_chars(self, array)
     |      Set the characters used to draw lines between rows and columns
     |
     |      - the array should contain 4 fields:
     |
     |          [horizontal, vertical, corner, header]
     |
     |      - default is set to:
     |
     |          ['-', '|', '+', '=']
     |
     |  set_cols_align(self, array)
     |      Set the desired columns alignment
     |
     |      - the elements of the array should be either "l", "c" or "r":
     |
     |          * "l": column flushed left
     |          * "c": column centered
     |          * "r": column flushed right
     |
     |  set_cols_dtype(self, array)
     |      Set the desired columns datatype for the cols.
     |
     |      - the elements of the array should be either a callable or any of
     |        "a", "t", "f", "e" or "i":
     |
     |          * "a": automatic (try to use the most appropriate datatype)
     |          * "t": treat as text
     |          * "f": treat as float in decimal format
     |          * "e": treat as float in exponential format
     |          * "i": treat as int
     |          * "b": treat as boolean
     |          * a callable: should return formatted string for any value given
     |
     |      - by default, automatic datatyping is used for each column
     |
     |  set_cols_valign(self, array)
     |      Set the desired columns vertical alignment
     |
     |      - the elements of the array should be either "t", "m" or "b":
     |
     |          * "t": column aligned on the top of the cell
     |          * "m": column aligned on the middle of the cell
     |          * "b": column aligned on the bottom of the cell
     |
     |  set_cols_width(self, array)
     |      Set the desired columns width
     |
     |      - the elements of the array should be integers, specifying the
     |        width of each column. For example:
     |
     |              [10, 20, 5]
     |
     |  set_deco(self, deco)
     |      Set the table decoration
     |
     |      - 'deco' can be a combination of:
     |
     |          Texttable.BORDER: Border around the table
     |          Texttable.HEADER: Horizontal line below the header
     |          Texttable.HLINES: Horizontal lines between rows
     |          Texttable.VLINES: Vertical lines between columns
     |
     |         All of them are enabled by default
     |
     |      - example:
     |
     |          Texttable.BORDER | Texttable.HEADER
     |
     |  set_header_align(self, array)
     |      Set the desired header alignment
     |
     |      - the elements of the array should be either "l", "c" or "r":
     |
     |          * "l": column flushed left
     |          * "c": column centered
     |          * "r": column flushed right
     |
     |  set_max_width(self, max_width)
     |      Set the maximum width of the table
     |
     |      - max_width is an integer, specifying the maximum width of the table
     |      - if set to 0, size is unlimited, therefore cells won't be wrapped
     |
     |  set_precision(self, width)
     |      Set the desired precision for float/exponential formats
     |
     |      - width must be an integer >= 0
     |
     |      - default value is set to 3
     |
     |  ----------------------------------------------------------------------
     |  Data and other attributes defined here:
     |
     |  BORDER = 1
     |
     |  HEADER = 2
     |
     |  HLINES = 4
     |
     |  VLINES = 8

DATA
    __all__ = ['Texttable', 'ArraySizeError']
    __author__ = 'Gerome Fournier <jef(at)foutaise.org>'
    __credits__ = 'Jeff Kowalczyk:\n    - textwrap improved import\n ...at...
    __license__ = 'MIT'
    __version__ = '1.7.0'

VERSION
    1.7.0

AUTHOR
    Gerome Fournier <jef(at)foutaise.org>

CREDITS
    Jeff Kowalczyk:
        - textwrap improved import
        - comment concerning header output

    Anonymous:
        - add_rows method, for adding rows in one go

    Sergey Simonenko:
        - redefined len() function to deal with non-ASCII characters

    Roger Lew:
        - columns datatype specifications

    Brian Peterson:
        - better handling of unicode errors

    Frank Sachsenheim:
        - add Python 2/3-compatibility

    Maximilian Hils:
        - fix minor bug for Python 3 compatibility

    frinkelpi:
        - preserve empty lines
```

## Forks

* [latextable](https://github.com/JAEarly/latextable) is a fork of texttable that provide a LaTeX backend.

modules.name: texttable
documentation:
module to create simple ASCII tables


Example:

    table = Texttable()
    table.set_cols_align(["l", "r", "c"])
    table.set_cols_valign(["t", "m", "b"])
    table.add_rows([["Name", "Age", "Nickname"],
                    ["Mr\nXavier\nHuon", 32, "Xav'"],
                    ["Mr\nBaptiste\nClement", 1, "Baby"],
                    ["Mme\nLouise\nBourgeau", 28, "Lou\n\nLoue"]])
    print(table.draw())
    print()

    table = Texttable()
    table.set_deco(Texttable.HEADER)
    table.set_cols_dtype(['t',  # text
                          'f',  # float (decimal)
                          'e',  # float (exponent)
                          'i',  # integer
                          'a']) # automatic
    table.set_cols_align(["l", "r", "r", "r", "l"])
    table.add_rows([["text",    "float", "exp", "int", "auto"],
                    ["abcd",    "67",    654,   89,    128.001],
                    ["efghijk", 67.5434, .654,  89.6,  12800000000000000000000.00023],
                    ["lmn",     5e-78,   5e-78, 89.4,  .000000000000128],
                    ["opqrstu", .023,    5e+78, 92.,   12800000000000000000000]])
    print(table.draw())

Result:

    +----------+-----+----------+
    |   Name   | Age | Nickname |
    +==========+=====+==========+
    | Mr       |     |          |
    | Xavier   |  32 |          |
    | Huon     |     |   Xav'   |
    +----------+-----+----------+
    | Mr       |     |          |
    | Baptiste |   1 |          |
    | Clement  |     |   Baby   |
    +----------+-----+----------+
    | Mme      |     |   Lou    |
    | Louise   |  28 |          |
    | Bourgeau |     |   Loue   |
    +----------+-----+----------+

    text   float       exp      int     auto
    ===========================================
    abcd   67.000   6.540e+02   89    128.001
    efgh   67.543   6.540e-01   90    1.280e+22
    ijkl   0.000    5.000e-78   89    0.000
    mnop   0.023    5.000e+78   92    1.280e+22
texttable - module to create simple ASCII tables
Copyright (C) 2003-2023 Gerome Fournier <jef(at)foutaise.org>

functions.name: texttable.uchar_width
documentation: Return the rendering width of a unicode character

functions.name: texttable.uchar_width
documentation: Return the rendering width of a unicode character

functions.name: texttable.obj2unicode
documentation: Return a unicode representation of a python object

functions.name: texttable.len
documentation: Redefining len here so it will be able to work with non-ASCII characters

classes.name: texttable.ArraySizeError
documentation: Exception raised when specified rows don't fit the required size

classes.name: texttable.FallbackToText
documentation: Used for failed conversion to float

methods.name: texttable.Texttable.__init__
documentation:
Constructor

- max_width is an integer, specifying the maximum width of the table
- if set to 0, size is unlimited, therefore cells won't be wrapped

methods.name: texttable.Texttable.reset
documentation:
Reset the instance

- reset rows and header

methods.name: texttable.Texttable.set_max_width
documentation:
Set the maximum width of the table

- max_width is an integer, specifying the maximum width of the table
- if set to 0, size is unlimited, therefore cells won't be wrapped

methods.name: texttable.Texttable.set_chars
documentation:
Set the characters used to draw lines between rows and columns

- the array should contain 4 fields:

    [horizontal, vertical, corner, header]

- default is set to:

    ['-', '|', '+', '=']

methods.name: texttable.Texttable.set_deco
documentation:
Set the table decoration

- 'deco' can be a combination of:

    Texttable.BORDER: Border around the table
    Texttable.HEADER: Horizontal line below the header
    Texttable.HLINES: Horizontal lines between rows
    Texttable.VLINES: Vertical lines between columns

   All of them are enabled by default

- example:

    Texttable.BORDER | Texttable.HEADER

methods.name: texttable.Texttable.set_header_align
documentation:
Set the desired header alignment

- the elements of the array should be either "l", "c" or "r":

    * "l": column flushed left
    * "c": column centered
    * "r": column flushed right

methods.name: texttable.Texttable.set_cols_align
documentation:
Set the desired columns alignment

- the elements of the array should be either "l", "c" or "r":

    * "l": column flushed left
    * "c": column centered
    * "r": column flushed right

methods.name: texttable.Texttable.set_cols_valign
documentation:
Set the desired columns vertical alignment

- the elements of the array should be either "t", "m" or "b":

    * "t": column aligned on the top of the cell
    * "m": column aligned on the middle of the cell
    * "b": column aligned on the bottom of the cell

methods.name: texttable.Texttable.set_cols_dtype
documentation:
Set the desired columns datatype for the cols.

- the elements of the array should be either a callable or any of
  "a", "t", "f", "e", "i" or "b":

    * "a": automatic (try to use the most appropriate datatype)
    * "t": treat as text
    * "f": treat as float in decimal format
    * "e": treat as float in exponential format
    * "i": treat as int
    * "b": treat as boolean
    * a callable: should return formatted string for any value given

- by default, automatic datatyping is used for each column

methods.name: texttable.Texttable.set_cols_width
documentation:
Set the desired columns width

- the elements of the array should be integers, specifying the
  width of each column. For example:

        [10, 20, 5]

methods.name: texttable.Texttable.set_precision
documentation:
Set the desired precision for float/exponential formats

- width must be an integer >= 0

- default value is set to 3

methods.name: texttable.Texttable.header
documentation: Specify the header of the table

methods.name: texttable.Texttable.add_row
documentation:
Add a row in the rows stack

- cells can contain newlines and tabs

methods.name: texttable.Texttable.add_rows
documentation:
Add several rows in the rows stack

- The 'rows' argument can be either an iterator returning arrays,
  or a by-dimensional array
- 'header' specifies if the first row should be used as the header
  of the table

methods.name: texttable.Texttable.draw
documentation:
Draw the table

- the table is returned as a whole string

methods.name: texttable.Texttable._fmt_int
documentation: Integer formatting class-method.

methods.name: texttable.Texttable._fmt_float
documentation:
Float formatting class-method.

- x parameter is ignored. Instead kw-argument f being x float-converted
  will be used.

- precision will be taken from `n` kw-argument.

methods.name: texttable.Texttable._fmt_exp
documentation:
Exponential formatting class-method.

- x parameter is ignored. Instead kw-argument f being x float-converted
  will be used.

- precision will be taken from `n` kw-argument.

methods.name: texttable.Texttable._fmt_text
documentation: String formatting class-method.

methods.name: texttable.Texttable._fmt_bool
documentation: Boolean formatting class-method

methods.name: texttable.Texttable._fmt_auto
documentation: auto formatting class-method.

methods.name: texttable.Texttable._str
documentation:
Handles string formatting of cell data

i - index of the cell datatype in self._dtype
x - cell data to format

methods.name: texttable.Texttable._check_row_size
documentation: Check that the specified array fits the previous rows size

methods.name: texttable.Texttable._has_vlines
documentation: Return a boolean, if vlines are required or not

methods.name: texttable.Texttable._has_hlines
documentation: Return a boolean, if hlines are required or not

methods.name: texttable.Texttable._has_border
documentation: Return a boolean, if border is required or not

methods.name: texttable.Texttable._has_header
documentation: Return a boolean, if header line is required or not

methods.name: texttable.Texttable._hline_header
documentation: Print header's horizontal line

methods.name: texttable.Texttable._hline
documentation: Print an horizontal line

methods.name: texttable.Texttable._build_hline
documentation:
Return a string used to separated rows or separate header from
rows

methods.name: texttable.Texttable._len_cell
documentation:
Return the width of the cell

Special characters are taken into account to return the width of the
cell, such like newlines and tabs

methods.name: texttable.Texttable._compute_cols_width
documentation:
Return an array with the width of each column

If a specific width has been specified, exit. If the total of the
columns width exceed the table desired width, another width will be
computed to fit, and cells will be wrapped.

methods.name: texttable.Texttable._check_align
documentation: Check if alignment has been specified, set default one if not

methods.name: texttable.Texttable._draw_line
documentation:
Draw a line

Loop over a single cell length, over all the cells

methods.name: texttable.Texttable._splitit
documentation:
Split each element of line to fit the column width

Each element is turned into a list, result of the wrapping of the
string to the desired width
